item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Is the image missing for anyone else or just me?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
If I could like comments I so would.
The uses abilities that they had discovered so far was meant to suggest the shear variety that it had (and hint that there was more that the Foundation doesn't know about), but I totally see the point.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Krusty the Klown voice: "Hey hey kids! Do you want… basically a normal refrigerator? Because this… is one of those! Huh huh hoo hoo heh heh! Store all your vegetables! And, uh… know which way is north? You can… you can take your raw ingredients with you wherever you go! And, uh… have… have fun… with… uh… heavy candle light?" *wipes sweat off brow* "Ugh. I shoulda thought this one through. This isn't Wondertastic at all!"

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I like the idea and the amount of work put into this (you still have an SCP-Fridge in that table, though). The MTF making a mistake during recovery is a very realistic touch. Definite +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Original, needs a little bit more polish, but still a good start. +1

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded: So you're saying that you created the image? You took the picture? In that case you are the copyright holder of the image, and posting it to the wiki, it is licensed as CC-BY-SA 3.0. In the future you should make this clear in the author post.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I would, but I couldn't get anyone on site to read my draft so I got people IRL. It meant that some gramatical errors slipped through, also I'm Aussie so there's going to be some spelling differences. I edited it to fix most of the stuff I've been told of now so… should be good.

item.title: Re: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
Source: Me
License: IDK, and I've been looking at legal stuff for the past 10 minutes and i don't have a bloody clue how this works. I created this image myself, and it's uploaded to imgur. Don't know what I'm meant to do next.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I really like this conceptually, but it feels unpolished. Spelling errors, lots of SCP-Fridges, etc.. I'd delete, polish it up more, then repost, so it shows up on Most Recently created again and people will give it a second look.

item.title: Junior Staff Post
item.content:encoded:
The image used in this article is unsourced and therefore may be non-compliant with the SCP wiki’s license (CC-BY-SA 3.0). Please provide the sources for the image. If it’s cc-compliant, it will remain. If it isn’t, it’ll be removed.

EDIT: The image has been sourced and is cc-compliant.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
It's an interesting concept, and has some plot points I liked (the idea of an MTF missing an anomaly check is believable and a bit humanizing). There's a lot of potential here, but this draft now has significant flaws that make me want to downvote.

Primarily, the article has a bunch of grammatical, spelling, and tonal issues (also you forgot an XXX). In addition, there are a handful of weirdly phrased sentences or stylistic choices like needlessly redacting expletives that suggest a lack of care or attention to detail.

But, like I said, the idea has great potential to be structured well and make a good mainlister. For now, novote.

item.title: First SCP!
item.content:encoded:
I thought it was about time some explanation was given as to how GOIs get their product out there. Some of you would probably just say "anomalously" but I think fridge backpack is more fun.

Image
Author: Me
License: Public Domain
Note: It's not really obvious but I hid an SOS in the fridge magnets. take from that what you will.
